stage.title: Docs + test strategy
stage.objective: Emit ONE per-feature specification `.md` for the feature under design, reusing /tmux:plan's existing spec-section vocabulary (Intent, Boundaries & Constraints, I/O & Edge-Case Matrix, Acceptance, Design Notes) — never a new doc format. Every section is GROUNDED in the upstream context blocks: Stage 0's resolved SIGNALS+TOPOLOGY, Stage 1's context dossier, and Stage 2's approved ADR — no project-shaped value is fabricated or hardcoded. This shard is the Stage 3 ENTRY (the "docs" half): it owns Intent through Acceptance + Design Notes, keeps the spec's Test Plan section an explicit STUB, and then routes into the existing sibling `stage-3b-test-strategy.xml` (the test-strategy half) to fill that stub. The two compose into one feature spec without overlap.

substep.title: Consume Stage 0 / 1 / 2 context — never re-probe
substep.action: Read the upstream emitted context blocks verbatim — do NOT re-probe the filesystem, re-run `tmux-cli rules resolve`, or re-open the architecture decision. Every fact this stage needs was resolved upstream:

From the Stage 0 capability block: SIGNALS (lang, framework, run_target, has_database, has_frontend, frontend_mode, n_auth_flows), E2E_RUNNER_AVAILABLE, and TOPOLOGY (source roots, layer names, root namespace, quality-gate commands). These ground the spec's Boundaries, Code Map, and Dependencies in this project's actual stack.

From the Stage 1 context dossier: the existing-system surfaces the feature touches (affected modules, call sites, data shapes, prior-art conventions). These ground Intent, the I/O & Edge-Case Matrix, and Dependencies.

From the Stage 2 approved ADR: the user-confirmed architecture decision (the chosen approach, its boundaries, the rejected alternatives and why). This is the AUTHORITATIVE design anchor — the emitted spec's Approach and Design Notes restate it, never re-decide it.

From the Stage 2 approved-decision block: TESTS_MODE (`required` | `off`) — whether tests are a deliverable this run. Carry it forward to substep 3.3 / Stage 3b (it shapes the Test Plan: a test deliverable when `required`, an eval-only coverage map when `off`). It does NOT change any other section — Boundaries, Acceptance, and the code-rule grounding are identical in both modes.

From the Stage 2 approved-decision block: the CITED EXEMPLAR(s) + naming pattern, the STYLE MANDATE (preserve existing even if imperfect), the GUIDANCE DOCS, and the build LOCATION they dictate. These ground the spec's Design Notes (the prior-art lineage), its Dependencies/build-location, and the concrete naming the implementation must follow — the spec mirrors the exemplar, never re-styles it.

check.condition: an upstream block (Stage 2 ADR or Stage 1 dossier) is absent from context
check.action: Note the gap IN the affected section rather than fabricating a value — resolve per the autonomous highest-quality-in-scope rule and record the rationale. Never invent a project fact to fill a missing block.

substep.title: Emit the feature spec .md using /tmux:plan's section vocabulary
substep.action: Write a per-feature specification `.md` under the command's research-root, reusing /tmux:plan's `<spec-deliverable-template>` section names VERBATIM — do NOT invent a new doc format. The docs half owns these sections:
substep.section: **Problem:** one-to-two sentences — what the feature adds/changes in the existing system (from the Stage 1 dossier). **Approach:** one-to-two sentences — the high-level "what" from the Stage 2 approved ADR, not a re-decision.

Three tiers — **Always:** invariants the feature must obey (grounded in the resolved TOPOLOGY conventions); **Ask First:** decidable forks the implementation worker auto-resolves to the highest-quality in-scope option (escalate ONLY user-only facts); **Never:** explicit non-goals from the ADR's rejected alternatives.

Optional — table (Scenario | Input / State | Expected Output / Behavior | Error Handling) covering happy path, error cases, boundary conditions, each row specific enough to become a test case. If the feature is pure-logic with no meaningful I/O surface, DELETE the section entirely per plan.xml's optional-section rule — do NOT write "N/A".

Which existing modules must already be in place and what they provide (from the Stage 1 dossier + Stage 0 topology); "none" if independent. State the build LOCATION the feature lands in, justified by the GUIDANCE DOCS (cite the AGENTS.md/CLAUDE.md/ADR path that dictates it).

Architectural rationale from the approved ADR (why this approach over the rejected ones); keep golden examples to 5–10 lines. REQUIRED prior-art lineage (do NOT delete this part even if the rest is straightforward): name the EXEMPLAR (the existing similar feature this is modeled on, with file refs), the concrete NAMING pattern mirrored (`role` → `name pattern`), and the STYLE MANDATE — "preserve the exemplar's existing style even where imperfect; resolved rule/lint gates still bind; gate-violating quirks are the only divergence". When EXEMPLARS = net-new, state "no analogue — grounded in resolved conventions, best-effort" instead.

STUB ONLY. Write an explicit, visible placeholder — e.g. "_Filled by Stage 3b (stage-3b-test-strategy.xml): one Test Plan row per acceptance unit, classified and mapped to {layer, driver, test path} from the Stage 0 capability signals._" — NOT a populated plan and NEVER the bare word "N/A". When TESTS_MODE=off, label the stub as the eval-only coverage map ("_Filled by Stage 3b — TESTS_MODE=off: eval-only coverage map, no test deliverable; all non-test gates still apply._") — still a visible stub for 3b, never "N/A". This section is deliberately left for the test-strategy half to complete; substep 3.3 hands the feature's acceptance criteria to it.

Acceptance criteria in Given/When/Then, checkbox-formatted (`- [ ] Given … when … then …`), each criterion independently testable and grounded in the ADR's expected behavior. These ARE the acceptance units Stage 3b classifies into Test Plan rows.
substep.rule: PORTABILITY: every value written into the spec is a resolved signal / topology value / dossier fact / ADR decision — NEVER a hardcoded source root, layer name, runner name, port, project name, or path literal. Emit no project name anywhere.
Section headings reuse /tmux:plan's vocabulary verbatim — do NOT rename, do NOT invent sibling sections. The docs half is Intent → Acceptance + Design Notes; the Test Plan stays a stub for 3b.

substep.title: Route into stage-3b-test-strategy.xml to fill the Test Plan stub
substep.load: After the spec `.md` is written, READ stage-3b-test-strategy.xml NOW and execute its content as the test-strategy half of this stage: hand it the emitted spec's Acceptance criteria (the acceptance units), the Stage 0 capability context block, AND TESTS_MODE so it renders the Test Plan table (a test deliverable when `required`, an eval-only coverage map when `off`) and replaces the docs half's Test Plan stub in place. It is present on disk (shipped with F5).

check.condition: stage-3b-test-strategy.xml is somehow absent from disk
check.action: The spec `.md` still emits with its Test Plan STUB intact and visibly labeled for Stage 3b — print "Stage 3b pending — feature/stage-3b-test-strategy.xml not present; Test Plan left as stub" and STOP gracefully. Never crash, never improvise the Test Plan body.

substep.action: Hand the completed feature spec (docs half + Stage 3b's Test Plan) to Stage 4 for implementation. Proceed to Stage 4.

execution-rules.rule: REUSE, DON'T REINVENT: the emitted spec reuses /tmux:plan's `<spec-deliverable-template>` section vocabulary verbatim — this shard EMITS a doc shaped by that vocabulary; it never re-implements /tmux:plan's planning, dispatch, or supervisor logic.

DOCS/TEST-STRATEGY SPLIT: stage-3-docs.xml owns Intent → Acceptance + Design Notes and keeps the spec's Test Plan an explicit stub; stage-3b-test-strategy.xml owns the Test Plan. The two compose into one feature spec without overlap — the stub is the handoff seam, never an empty "N/A".

PORTABILITY: every spec value is a resolved Stage 0 signal / TOPOLOGY value / Stage 1 dossier fact / Stage 2 ADR decision — NEVER a hardcoded source root, layer name, runner name, port, path literal, or project name.
GROUNDED, NOT FABRICATED: when an upstream block is missing, the affected section records the gap and resolves per the highest-quality in-scope rule — it never invents a project fact.
AUTONOMOUS STAGE: resolve every decidable fork to the highest-quality in-scope option and record the rationale; never block on the user (feature.xml is interactive ONLY at Stage 2).
execution-rules.error-reporting: On a genuine harness/infra/spec defect (an MCP tool or `tmux-cli` command that errored after its own retries, a contradictory/under-specified goal spec, or a validator-error-class condition), follow the shared `error-reporting` procedure in `tmux/task-report.xml` — compose + submit autonomously, honoring api.enabled / DEDUP / RECURSION-guard. NEVER on expected work failures (red validate cycle, supervisor pushback, NEED_INPUT, unwritten-code test red) or daemon-owned goal-failures.

llm.critical-rules: • AUTONOMOUS stage — resolve every decidable fork to the highest-quality in-scope option and record the rationale; never block on the user (mirrors feature.xml's autonomous-stage mandate). • REUSE /tmux:plan's spec-section vocabulary VERBATIM (Intent, Boundaries & Constraints, I/O & Edge-Case Matrix, Acceptance, Design Notes) — never invent a new doc format; the shard EMITS a doc, it does not run a planning supervisor. • GROUND every section in Stage 0 SIGNALS+TOPOLOGY, the Stage 1 dossier, and the Stage 2 approved ADR — never re-probe, never re-decide the architecture, never fabricate a missing fact. • PRIOR-ART LINEAGE IS REQUIRED in Design Notes — name the EXEMPLAR (the existing similar feature modeled on), the NAMING pattern mirrored, the STYLE MANDATE (preserve existing even if imperfect; resolved gates still bind), and the build LOCATION per the GUIDANCE DOCS. Net-new → "no analogue — resolved conventions, best-effort". • PORTABILITY IS MANDATORY — no source root, layer name, runner name, port, path literal, or project name is hardcoded; every value is a resolved signal or upstream-block fact. • The spec's Test Plan section is a VISIBLE STUB labeled for Stage 3b — never a populated plan, never "N/A"; substep 3.3 routes into stage-3b-test-strategy.xml to fill it. • A missing stage-3b shard stops gracefully with the spec's Test Plan stub intact — never crash, never improvise the Test Plan body.
